zh.Abbey Dore, Herefordshire, England: 英國賀福爾艾比 多雷
zh.Holy Trinity and St Mary Churchyard, Abb: 英國賀福爾艾比 多雷
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William de Grandison I於1255年出生在英國奧可福爾卡希頓時，他的父親Pierre I Sire De Grandison66歲，母親Agnes Neufchâtel46歲。他於1285年之前與Lady Sibyl de Tregoz在英國薩默塞特多亞特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1295年登記服兵役。他於1298年被列為member of parliament。他去世於1335年6月27日在法國加斯可享年80歲，埋葬在英國賀福爾艾比 多雷。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William de Grandison I于1255年出生在英国奧可福爾卡希頓时，他的父亲Pierre I Sire De Grandison66岁，母亲Agnes Neufchâtel46岁。他于1285年之前与Lady Sibyl de Tregoz在英国薩默塞特多亞特结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他于1295年登记服兵役。他于1298年被列为member of parliament。他去世于1335年6月27日在法国加斯可享年80岁，埋葬在英国賀福爾艾比 多雷。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Grandison
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): said to be a habitational name from Granson on Lake Neuchâtel (Switzerland).

History: The first known bearer of this surname is Rigaldus de Grancione (flourished 1040). The name was taken to Britain by Otes de Grandison (died 1328) and his brother. They were among a group of Savoyards who settled in England when Henry III married a granddaughter of the Count of Savoy.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de Grandison I was born in 1255, in Cassington, Oxfordshire, England, his father, Pierre I Sire De Grandison, was 66 and his mother, Agnes Neufchâtel, was 46. He married Lady Sibyl de Tregoz before 1285, in Donyatt, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He registered for military service in 1295. In 1298, his occupation is listed as member of parliament. He died on 27 June 1335, in Gascogne, France, at the age of 80, and was buried in Abbey Dore, Herefordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William de Grandison I est né en 1255, à Cassington, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Pierre I Sire De Grandison, avait 66 ans et sa mère, Agnes Neufchâtel, avait 46 ans. Il a épousé Lady Sibyl de Tregoz avant 1285, à Donyatt, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1295. En 1298, sa profession de son indiquée est member of parliament. Il est décédé le 27 juin 1335, à Gascogne, France, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterré à Abbey Dore, Herefordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William de Grandison I 1255 in Cassington, Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Pierre I Sire De Grandison 66 und seine Mutter Agnes Neufchâtel 46. Er hat vor 1285 in Donyatt, Somerset, England Lady Sibyl de Tregoz geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er hat sich 1295 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1298 ist sein Beruf als member of parliament aufgeführt. Er starb am 27. Juni 1335 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Gascogne, Frankreich und wurde in Abbey Dore, Herefordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de Grandison I è nato il nel 1255 nel Cassington, Oxfordshire, England, il suo padre, Pierre I Sire De Grandison, aveva 66 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Neufchâtel, aveva 46 anni. Lui sposato Lady Sibyl de Tregoz prima del 1285, nel Donyatt, Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1295. Nel 1298, la sua occupazione è indicata come member of parliament. Morì il 27 giugno 1335, nel Gascogne, Francia, all'età di 80 anni, e fu sepolto a Abbey Dore, Herefordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William de Grandison Iが1255年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアカシントンで生まれた時，父Pierre I Sire De Grandisonは66歳，母Agnes Neufchâtelは46歳であった。1285年以前にイギリスソメルセトドニーアットでLady Sibyl de Tregozと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1295年に兵役登録をした。1298年，職業はmember of parliamentと記録されている。彼は1335年6月27日にフランスガスコグンで80歳で死去し，イギリスハーエフォードシュアイアアビー ドーアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de Grandison I(이)가 1255년 영국 스퍼드실르 캐징턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Pierre I Sire De Grandison(은)는 66세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Neufchâtel(은)는 46세였다. 그는 1285년 이전 영국 서머세트 도니엇에서 Lady Sibyl de Tregoz(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1295년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1298년, 그의 직업은 member of parliament이었다. 그는 1335년 6월 27일 프랑스 가스코그니에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허퍼드실르 애브브이 도어에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de Grandison I nasceu em 1255, em Cassington, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Pierre I Sire De Grandison, tinha 66 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Neufchâtel, tinha 46 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Sibyl de Tregoz antes 1285, em Donyatt, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1295. Em 1298, sua profissão aparece como member of parliament. Ele faleceu em 27 de junho de 1335, em Gascogne, França, com 80 anos, e foi sepultado em Abbey Dore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William de Grandison I родился в 1255 г. в местности кассингтон, оксфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Pierre I Sire De Grandison было 66 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Neufchâtel, 46. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Sibyl de Tregoz перед 1285 г. в местности донятт, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. В 1295 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1298 годуработал по профессии member of parliament. Он умер 27 июня 1335 г. в местности гаскогн, Франция в возрасте 80 лет и был похоронен в местности эбби дор, хирефордшир, Англия.

sm.between 1298 and 1325: mai le 1298 e o'o I le 1325

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William de Grandison I nació en 1255, en Cassington, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Pierre I Sire De Grandison, tenía 66 años y su mamá, Agnes Neufchâtel, tenía 46 años. Se casó con Lady Sibyl de Tregoz antes de 1285, en Donyatt, Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1295. En 1298, su ocupación registrada era member of parliament. Murió el 27 de junio de 1335, en Gascogne, Francia, a la edad de 80 años, y fue sepultado en Abbey Dore, Herefordshire, Inglaterra.
